title: Three-tier automation map — fully-automatic / one-click / manual
html:
Refined version of the Mar 12 automation matrix, now formalized as the Rankett fulfillment playbook with three tiers. FULLY AUTOMATIC (runs itself, nobody clicks): AI Visibility Audit (10-day cron auto-scoring, auto-completes the card), Query Tracking (derived from analysis), Monthly Plan Generation (1st-of-month cron creates all client cards), llms.txt (templated from AI Profile, auto-deployed), Crawlability Check (foundational scorer crawls HTTPS/robots/sitemap/schema/H1/meta/AI-crawler access → pass/fail report card), Blog Refresh (auto-update stats/dates on published posts). ONE-CLICK + REVIEW: Blog Posts (Claude generates AEO HTML → admin reviews → deploy as WordPress draft), Schema Markup (Claude JSON-LD → spot-check → copy/deploy), FAQ Optimization (Q&A HTML + FAQPage schema → review → deploy), Answer Snippets/TLDR (targeted answer boxes → review → place). MANUAL (dashboard tracks status): GBP Optimization (Google login, NAP accuracy), Citation Building (Yelp / BBB / Featured.com submissions), YouTube Videos (admin creates/sources), Third-Party Authority (admin pitches guest posts), Semantic Refresh (admin audits keywords/meta/internal links), ROI Reporting (visibility-to-revenue impact, National tier only).

TL;DR

  • Three-tier Rankett fulfillment taxonomy: fully automatic (6 tasks), one-click + review (4 tasks via Claude), manual (6 tasks tracked in dashboard).
  • Auto-deploy of llms.txt direct to client sites is now the cron architecture, not just a one-off.
  • "AI Profile" is the canonical client-data store templated into both llms.txt and schema markup.
  • ROI Reporting reserved for National tier — first explicit mention of a tier above Partner Plan.
  • Citation building names the directory targets: yelp, BBB, Featured.com.

rankett · autonomous fulfillment engine · anthropic claude · ai visibility engine · llm txt · schema markup for ai · ghost white label · wordpress
